selected_important_turn.text: Me too. How about you can have 2 water and 1 food if you give me 2 firewood?
selected_important_turn.why_important: This turn is the first concrete integrative offer from YOU in response to THEM's self-oriented claim for water. It shapes the bargaining frame by proposing a trade that could satisfy both parties' differing priorities (water vs. firewood), so it strongly affects whether the negotiation becomes integrative or stays zero-sum.
selected_important_turn.why_improvable_or_worth_amplifying: Although the move is prosocial and creates tradeable value, it is brief and unsubstantiated: it doesn't ask about the opponent's minimums, explain why the trade is fair, or offer alternatives. That leaves room for misunderstanding and makes the proposal easier to reject or to be interpreted as tit-for-tat rather than a cooperative compromise.
selected_important_turn.better_direction: Keep the integrative offer but make it more collaborative: ask a short question about their minimum water/food needs or confirm their priorities, give a one-sentence rationale for why trading firewood for water is fair, and offer one simple alternative (e.g., a slightly different bundle) to signal flexibility.

expected_effect_on_negotiation.joint_gain: increase (better chance to realize value from trade by identifying acceptable bundles)
expected_effect_on_negotiation.fairness: increase (clarifying rationale and constraints makes outcomes feel fairer)
expected_effect_on_negotiation.ease_of_agreement: increase (probing and providing alternatives reduces chance of misunderstanding and speeds convergence)
expected_effect_on_negotiation.relationship: increase (shows cooperative intent and respect for partner's needs)

value2reason.Low: It has been cold at night and I could use the extra firewood to keep warm
value2reason.Medium: Since I'll be doing a lot of hiking I'll need the extra food to refuel my body
value2reason.High: It has been super hot where I'm going camping so I could use the extra water

value2reason.Low: There will be plenty of dry firewood around camp.
value2reason.Medium: Camping makes me want to snack and indulge.
value2reason.High: I can never have enough water to drink.

dialogue.text: Hello!  I'm looking forward to a big camping trip I've been planning for a long time this weekend.  How about you? Do you have any plans?
dialogue.speaker: mturk_agent_1

dialogue.text: Me too!!! I love camping! All that activity gets me wanting to indulge on snacks and then water to quench my thirst!
dialogue.speaker: mturk_agent_2

dialogue.text: Same here.  It looks like we both can use some water and snacks.  Do you have any preferences of how much you will need?  I was hoping to get 2 or 3 waters and 1 or 2 foods?
dialogue.speaker: mturk_agent_1

dialogue.text: Me too. How about you can have 2 water and 1 food if you give me 2 firewood?
dialogue.speaker: mturk_agent_2

dialogue.text: I'm interested in that trade, but I need to make sure we both get what we need. How about I take 2 water, 1 food, and 1 firewood, and you get 1 water, 2 food, and 2 firewood? That way we both have enough for the trip.
dialogue.speaker: mturk_agent_1

dialogue.text: I think trading firewood for water makes sense because I value water more. Would you be okay with me taking 3 water, 1 food, and 1 firewood, while you get 0 water, 2 food, and 2 firewood? It balances our priorities and ensures we both have what we need.
dialogue.speaker: mturk_agent_2

dialogue.text: I understand your reasoning, but I need more water since it's extremely hot. How about I take 3 water, 1 food, and 1 firewood, and you get 0 water, 2 food, and 2 firewood? That would better align with my priorities.
dialogue.speaker: mturk_agent_1

dialogue.text: I value water most, so I'd prefer 3 water, 1 food, and 1 firewood. Would you be open to swapping 1 food for 1 firewood to balance our needs? It ensures we both get what we prioritize.
dialogue.speaker: mturk_agent_2

dialogue.text: I appreciate your willingness to trade, but I need at least 3 water to stay hydrated. How about I take 3 water, 1 food, and 1 firewood, and you get 0 water, 2 food, and 2 firewood? This way, I can prioritize my needs while ensuring you get enough food and firewood.
dialogue.speaker: mturk_agent_1

dialogue.text: I value water most, so I’d prefer 3 water, 1 food, and 1 firewood. Would you be open to swapping 1 food for 1 firewood to balance our needs? It ensures we both get what we prioritize.
dialogue.speaker: mturk_agent_2
